summary: Changed the preprocessor condition guarding the custom `STATIC_ASSERT` macro in `src/Runtime/platform.h` from `#ifdef _Static_assert` to `#ifdef __MWERKS__`. This makes the fallback struct-based assertion path explicitly depend on the Metrowerks compiler rather than testing for `_Static_assert`, avoiding the wrong branch selection when `_Static_assert` has been aliased or defined in the surrounding compatibility code.
what_changed: In `src/Runtime/platform.h`, the `STATIC_ASSERT(cond)` macro guard was changed from `#ifdef _Static_assert` to `#ifdef __MWERKS__`.
The custom `STATIC_ASSERT` implementation shown in the diff remains a struct containing `int x[1 - 2 * !(cond)]`; only the condition selecting it changed.
The PR was very small: one file changed, one line added, one line deleted, with no PR body or review discussion captured.
smart_moves: Used a compiler-identity macro, `__MWERKS__`, for compiler-specific behavior instead of testing `_Static_assert`, which may be affected by compatibility aliases such as `#define _Static_assert static_assert`.
Kept the fix minimal and localized to the platform compatibility header, reducing risk to unrelated decompilation code.
decomp_lessons: When writing portability shims for decomp projects, distinguish language feature availability from target compiler compatibility. A macro like `_Static_assert` can be defined by the project, while `__MWERKS__` directly indicates the compiler path needing special handling.
Compiler-specific compatibility code should generally be gated on compiler macros when the implementation relies on behavior or syntax accepted by that compiler.
Small header fixes can matter broadly because platform headers affect many translation units and build configurations.
naming_conventions: `STATIC_ASSERT(cond)` is the project-facing macro name for compile-time assertions in this header.
`__MWERKS__` is used as the Metrowerks compiler detection macro.
`_Static_assert` may be aliased to `static_assert` in compatibility code, so it should not be treated as a reliable compiler detector.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or function matching tactics were present in the captured PR evidence.
The relevant tactic is build-compatibility hygiene: keep assertion macros usable under the target Metrowerks toolchain without perturbing source-level decomp work.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review approvals were included in the captured PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find other uses of `_Static_assert` or `STATIC_ASSERT` that may be incorrectly guarded by feature macros instead of compiler macros.
Check `src/Runtime/platform.h` compatibility paths for other Metrowerks-specific definitions gated by indirect aliases.
Search for additional `__MWERKS__` conditions to ensure compiler-specific shims are consistently isolated.
